extra.reasoning: rec_drug_b is set true only by r1 (IF bradycardia). rec_drug_a is set true only by r4 (IF bradycardia AND low_ejection_fraction AND hyperkalemia). These rules write to different outputs, so the per-output priority conflict resolution never suppresses either one. When bradycardia, low_ejection_fraction, and hyperkalemia are all true, r1 fires (rec_drug_b=true) and r4 fires (rec_drug_a=true) simultaneously, making both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b true. This directly violates the mutual-exclusion property NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). heart_failure and prior_gi_bleed only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant; setting them false keeps the counterexample minimal.
